items.title: Genie 3: A New Frontier for World Models — Google DeepMind
items.author: deepmind.google
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been highly active in early 2026 with several major research breakthroughs. The most prominent is Gemini Deep Think (also referred to as Gemini 3.1 Deep Think), a specialized AI model mode designed to accelerate scientific discovery across mathematics, physics, chemistry, and computer science. In January 2026, it significantly outperformed prior versions on Olympiad-level problems, and DeepMind published two cross-disciplinary papers detailing its use by professional research

items.author: x.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind and Google AI have been active in April 2026 with several major announcements. Google DeepMind upgraded Gemini 3 Deep Think, its specialized reasoning mode designed to help solve modern science, research, and engineering challenges, with a demonstration from the Wang Lab at Duke University using it to design new semiconductor materials. The lab also announced a research partnership with Boston Dynamics to integrate Gemini Robotics foundational capabilities into the new Atlas huma

items.title: Vertex AI release notes | Generative AI on Vertex AI | Google Cloud Documentation
items.author: docs.cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has been actively releasing major Gemini model and platform updates in early 2026. The flagship announcement is Gemini 3.1 Pro, now available on Gemini CLI, Gemini Enterprise, and Vertex AI — described as a noticeably smarter baseline for complex reasoning and problem-solving. Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is in public preview as the most cost-efficient model optimized for high-volume, low-latency workloads. Google also introduced Gemini Enterprise, a dedicated enterprise tier

items.title: Gemini CLI Release Notes - April 2026
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet:
The google-gemini GitHub organization has been actively releasing updates in early 2026, with the gemini-cli project being the most active repository. Version 0.36.0 was released on April 1, 2026, introducing a multi-registry architecture and native macOS Seatbelt and Windows sandboxing for enhanced subagent security. The project follows a structured release cadence: nightly builds at UTC 00:00 daily, preview releases every Tuesday at UTC 23:59, and stable releases every Tuesday at UTC 20:00.

A

items.title: Gemini API Image Generation | Google AI for Developers
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Imagen 4 is Google's latest and highest-quality text-to-image generation model, available through two main access paths: the Gemini API (via Google AI Studio / AI for Developers) and Vertex AI. The model ID is `imagen-4.0-generate-001` and it supports generating images up to 2K resolution with photorealistic output, legible text rendering in images (recommended under 25 characters), and five aspect ratios. It is accessible via the Python SDK using `from google import genai` and calling `client.m

items.title: Build apps in Google AI Studio | Gemini API
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Google AI Studio launched a full-stack vibe coding experience in March 2026, representing a major evolution of the platform. The update integrates the Antigravity coding agent, which autonomously plans projects, writes multi-file code, installs dependencies, runs tests, and iterates on errors — all from natural language prompts. The full-stack runtime supports a React frontend paired with a Node.js backend, enabling secure API key storage, npm package support, database connections, and multiplay

items.title: Genkit - Firebase Landing Page
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is Google's open-source AI framework for building production-ready AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified API layer across multiple AI model providers including Google Gemini, OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, and others, with built-in developer tooling such as a local Developer UI for testing and debugging flows. Core features include structured output generation, tool/function calling, streaming responses, multimodal input/output, an

items.title: GitHub - firebase/genkit: An open source framework for building AI-powered apps
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and used in production by Google, designed for building full-stack AI-powered applications in JavaScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified, code-centric interface for integrating AI models from providers like Google (Gemini), OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama, with built-in observability, evaluations, and a local developer UI. The framework is structured around 'flows' — typed, observable functions — and supports retrieval-augmented generation (RAG),

items.title: Learning pathways | Firebase - Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Studio is Google's agentic cloud-based development environment that integrates Gemini AI to help developers build full-stack applications including web, mobile, and backend systems. As of 2026, Firebase Studio has official documentation and a codelab tutorial covering how to get started, use templates, and write effective prompts for AI-assisted development. While Firebase Studio itself is primarily a full-stack app IDE, it supports AI-powered features via Firebase AI Logic (Gemini API)
